definitions.definition: A written or verbal account of facts, information, or events. It is a document that provides information about a particular subject or issue.
A type of report that is written in a formal tone and follows a specific structure and format. It is used to present information to a specific audience, such as management or stakeholders.
A type of report that is written in an informal tone and may not follow a specific structure or format. It is used to present information to a general audience or to facilitate discussion.
A brief summary of the main points of a report, usually included at the beginning. It provides an overview of the report's content and highlights the key findings.
The opening section of a report that provides background information and sets the context for the rest of the report.
The main section of a report that presents the main points, findings, and analysis.
The final section of a report that summarizes the main points and reiterates the key findings.
Suggestions or proposals for action based on the findings and analysis presented in the report.
A section of a report that includes additional information, such as supporting data, tables, or figures, that are not essential to the main report.
A brief summary of the report that provides an overview of the content and highlights the key findings.
The heading of a report that provides a clear and concise indication of its content.
The reason or objective for which a report is written.
The people for whom a report is written, including stakeholders, management, or the general public.
The style and structure of a report, including the use of headings, subheadings, and paragraphs.
The attitude or style of a report, including whether it is formal, informal, or neutral.
The words, phrases, and sentences used in a report, including their clarity, simplicity, and technicality.
The information and data presented in a report, including its relevance, accuracy, and completeness.
Graphics, charts, tables, and other visual elements used to support the content and make the report more engaging.
A list of the report's sections and subsections, including page numbers.
